feed.title: SAP Community - SAP BTP, ABAP environment
feed.subtitle: SAP BTP, ABAP environment Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How to create CR using SOAP BusinessPartnerSUITEBulkReplicateRequest_In
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Using Service ( BusinessPartnerSUITEBulkReplicateRequest_In ) can we save change request in Draft Mode? if yes what was the parameter need to be passed in the input level ?

And need to capture the the warning ,error or Success messages as well . can any one help me on the development.

SAP Master Data Governance 

SAP BTP, ABAP environment 

 

@markus_greutter 

@SAPABAP 

@MDGS

entry.title: Trial, BTP, RAP, APP Fiori, VSCode, OData V2: The request has been blocked by UCON.
entry.content:
After some updates in VScode and Fiori Extensions in VScode(Since 19/02/2024), when I tried to preview the APP Fiori, I received this popup asking the user and pw, but after I inserted the credentials the popup continues appearing and I get the error "The request has been blocked by UCON.The request has been blocked by UCON." (Before this update the preview worked fine.)

Someone is having this same issue? (When I do the preview RAP/OData V2 in eclipse it works fine)

entry.title: Services unavailable in business application studio, The destination is mis-configured
entry.content:
Dear All, 

I want to create a Fiori App by using BAS but I'm struggeling with setting up the system. Please see screenshot below. Maybe somebody can tell me what I'm missing here?

Here is also the destination in BTP:

An the setup in BAP:

 

Thanks in advance,

Philipp

entry.title: Any explain me bapi from ? Why we use ? When we use?
Use destination over VPN
entry.content:
I have a URL which is accessible from client VPN. I need to access that service using destination.

How do i create destination which is accessible from client VPN.

Any pointers on same ?

entry.title: Disable Create button using SAP RAP
entry.content:
Hello,

I am developing an app using restful ABAP where I have a parent entity as list report and child entity as line item (table) in the object page.
I am trying to disable the 'Create' button based on some conditions for the child entity. Following are the steps I followed.

1. In the projection view for child entity, I defined the following
@UI.createHidden: #(Hide_create)

2. Created a virtual field in the projection view
@ObjectModel.virtualElementCalculatedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_FIELD_CONFIG'
virtual Hide_create: abap_boolean

3. in class ZCL_FIELD_CONFIG, i am passing Hide_create = abap_true based on some conditions.

Here, the control is never going to the class ZCL_FIELD_CONFIG to calculate the Hide_create value.
But, if I give the annotation @UI.createHidden:true in the projection view, the create button is disabled.

Please let me know how can I disable it based on the condition.

entry.title: Business Application Studio doesn't work with AVG
entry.content:
Hello everyone, I installed the AVG antivirus on my machine, then accessed the BAS and tried to create a new project using templates. It presented me with the errors below when I debugged. I tested it on a machine without AVG and it worked. Has anyone experienced this situation before? Did you manage to resolve it without removing AVG? How?

Thank you.

entry.title: Connect SAP BAS to SAP Public Cloud Problem
entry.content:
I try to connect BAS to Public Cloud follow step tutorial https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-custom-ui-bas-connect-s4hc.html

The difference is my domain is xxx.sap.cloud and in tutorial is ondemand.com . But when I check Destination Connection in BAS, I got error  Unauthorized to access v4 catalog service, Unauthorized to access v2 catalog service. How can I fix it?

Thanks very much,

entry.title: Rise with SAP and SAP BTP and Cloud foundry: The Future for SAP Consultants
entry.content:
In the dynamic landscape of enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems, SAP stands tall as a leader, continually evolving to meet the changing demands of businesses across the globe. With the introduction of Rise with SAP, the company has taken a bold step forward, offering a comprehensive transformation as a service to help businesses become intelligent enterprises. This innovative solution has significant implications for SAP functional and technical consultants, promising to redefine their roles, enhance their skill set, and open up new avenues for growth. Let's delve deeper into what Rise with SAP entails and explore its impact on the consulting world.

Understanding Rise with SAP

Rise with SAP is not merely a product or a service; it's a comprehensive, holistic bundle designed to facilitate business transformation. It encompasses everything a company needs to transition into an intelligent enterprise, from software solutions and cloud services to a supportive business network and tools for process transformation. Essentially, Rise with SAP is about helping businesses migrate to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, leveraging state-of-the-art technologies, and embracing a cloud-centric infrastructure.

 

1. SAP S/4HANA Cloud: The core of the offering, providing a powerful ERP system that’s flexible, scalable, and capable of real-time analytics.
2. SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP): Enabling businesses to build, extend, and enhance applications with agility, using services like database and data management, analytics, application development, and integration.
3. SAP Business Network: Connecting businesses with millions of trading partners globally, this component facilitates efficient collaboration and commerce among businesses.
4. Business Process Intelligence: Tools and insights to understand, innovate, and transform processes to enhance efficiency and effectiveness.
5. Cloud Infrastructure and Services: Supporting the shift to a cloud environment with services offered through SAP's partnership with leading cloud providers.

The Role of SAP Consultants in the Rise Era

For SAP consultants, both functional and technical, Rise with SAP presents a plethora of opportunities and challenges. Here’s how:

- Upskilling and Reskilling:The move to cloud and the integration of new technologies necessitate constant learning. Consultants need to familiarize themselves with SAP’s Business Technology Platform and cloud-native development, among other areas.
- Process Transformation Expertise: With a focus on business process intelligence, consultants can play a crucial role in helping businesses understand their process inefficiencies and re-engineer them for better performance.
- Strategic Advisory: Consultants can act as strategic advisors to businesses, guiding them through their digital transformation journey, ensuring that they leverage all components of Rise with SAP effectively.
- Technical Migration Skills: For technical consultants, expertise in migrating existing ERP systems to SAP S/4HANA Cloud is vital. This includes understanding the nuances of cloud architecture, data migration, and ensuring seamless integration of legacy systems

 The Way Forward

Rise with SAP is more than just a new offering—it's a new paradigm that emphasizes flexibility, efficiency, and innovation. For SAP functional and technical consultants, it's an exciting time to be at the forefront of technological evolution, guiding businesses through their transformation journeys. Embracing continuous learning, adapting to new technologies, and understanding the strategic goals of businesses are key to thriving in this new era. As Rise with SAP continues to evolve, so too will the role of consultants, who will undoubtedly play a pivotal role in shaping the intelligent enterprises of tomorrow.

Empowering Enterprises: Rise with SAP and SAP BTP

In the ever-evolving landscape of digital transformation, businesses are constantly seeking innovative solutions to adapt, grow, and thrive. SAP, a global leader in enterprise software, has been at the forefront of this revolution, offering cutting-edge technologies and platforms to empower organizations worldwide. Two key pillars of SAP's strategy, "Rise with SAP" and "SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)," stand out as transformative forces driving the future of enterprise success.

Rise with SAP: Elevating Business Transformation

"Rise with SAP" represents a holistic approach to business transformation, providing end-to-end support for organizations seeking to navigate complex challenges and unlock new opportunities. At its core, Rise with SAP offers a comprehensive suite of services designed to streamline migration to the cloud, accelerate innovation, and optimize operations across the entire enterprise.

 Key Components of Rise with SAP:

1. SAP S/4HANA Cloud:As the digital core of Rise with SAP, SAP S/4HANA Cloud enables businesses to modernize their operations, streamline processes, and gain real-time insights for smarter decision-making. With built-in intelligence and advanced capabilities, S/4HANA Cloud serves as the foundation for digital transformation initiatives.

2. Business Process Intelligence:Leveraging AI-driven analytics, Rise with SAP provides deep insights into business processes, helping organizations identify areas for improvement, optimize workflows, and enhance operational efficiency.

3. Industry Cloud Solutions:Recognizing the unique challenges faced by different industries, Rise with SAP offers industry-specific cloud solutions tailored to meet the needs of sectors such as retail, manufacturing, healthcare, and more. These industry cloud solutions deliver specialized functionality and best practices to drive innovation and competitiveness.

4. SAP Business Network: Facilitating seamless collaboration and connectivity, SAP Business Network enables businesses to engage with customers, suppliers, and partners in a unified digital ecosystem. By fostering greater transparency and efficiency in supply chains, procurement, and commerce, the Business Network fuels growth and resilience.

 SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP): Enabling Innovation and Agility

Complementing Rise with SAP is the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), a comprehensive suite of tools and services designed to empower organizations to innovate, integrate, and extend their SAP solutions. As a flexible and scalable platform, SAP BTP enables businesses to build, deploy, and manage applications that drive digital transformation and unlock new value.

Key Capabilities of SAP BTP:

1. Application Development: With a rich set of development tools and frameworks, SAP BTP empowers organizations to create custom applications tailored to their unique needs. Whether building new solutions or extending existing ones, developers can leverage advanced technologies such as AI, IoT, and blockchain to deliver innovative experiences and drive business outcomes.

2. Integration Services: SAP BTP provides robust integration capabilities, allowing businesses to connect disparate systems and data sources seamlessly. By enabling real-time data exchange and process orchestration, integration services enhance agility, collaboration, and decision-making across the enterprise.

3. Analytics and Insights: Leveraging advanced analytics and machine learning capabilities, SAP BTP enables organizations to derive actionable insights from vast amounts of data. Whether analyzing customer behavior, optimizing operations, or predicting future trends, analytics services empower businesses to make data-driven decisions with confidence.

4. Extensibility and Flexibility:As businesses evolve and grow, SAP BTP offers the flexibility to adapt and scale accordingly. Whether deploying applications on-premises, in the cloud, or in a hybrid environment, organizations can leverage SAP BTP to meet changing business requirements and drive continuous innovation.

Conclusion: Embracing the Future of Enterprise Excellence

In an era of unprecedented change and disruption, the partnership between Rise with SAP and SAP BTP represents a powerful force for driving enterprise excellence. By leveraging the combined strengths of these two pillars, organizations can accelerate their journey towards digital transformation, unlock new value streams, and stay ahead of the competition in today's dynamic business landscape. With innovation at its core and agility as its foundation, SAP continues to empower businesses to rise to new heights of success in the digital age.

 

 Exploring the Synergy between SAP BTP and Cloud Foundry

In the realm of enterprise innovation and digital transformation, the synergy between SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and Cloud Foundry represents a powerful combination that enables organizations to build, deploy, and manage applications with unprecedented flexibility, scalability, and agility. As two leading platforms in their respective domains, SAP BTP and Cloud Foundry offer complementary capabilities that empower businesses to accelerate their journey towards modernization and unlock new opportunities for growth.

Understanding SAP BTP and Cloud Foundry

SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)

SAP BTP serves as a comprehensive platform-as-a-service (PaaS) offering from SAP, designed to provide organizations with the tools and services needed to innovate, integrate, and extend their SAP solutions. With a focus on agility, openness, and extensibility, SAP BTP enables businesses to leverage advanced technologies such as artificial intelligence (AI), Internet of Things (IoT), and machine learning to drive digital transformation and achieve strategic objectives.

 Cloud Foundry

Cloud Foundry is an open-source platform-as-a-service (PaaS) that enables developers to build, deploy, and manage applications across multiple cloud environments with ease. Known for its simplicity, flexibility, and portability, Cloud Foundry abstracts away the complexities of infrastructure management, allowing developers to focus on writing code and delivering value to end-users rapidly.

Leveraging the Benefits of SAP BTP with Cloud Foundry

 1. Agility and Flexibility:
- By integrating with Cloud Foundry, SAP BTP provides organizations with unparalleled agility and flexibility in application development and deployment. Developers can leverage Cloud Foundry's containerization and orchestration capabilities to deploy applications quickly and scale them effortlessly to meet changing business demands.

 2. Multi-Cloud Deployment:
- With Cloud Foundry's support for multi-cloud environments, organizations can deploy SAP BTP applications across a variety of cloud providers, including AWS, Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud Platform, and more. This flexibility enables businesses to avoid vendor lock-in and choose the cloud environment that best suits their requirements.

 3. Developer Productivity:
- Cloud Foundry's streamlined development experience and built-in automation tools empower developers to focus on writing code and delivering value without being bogged down by infrastructure concerns. By integrating with SAP BTP, developers can leverage a wide range of services and APIs to accelerate application development and enhance productivity.

 4. Scalability and Resilience:
- SAP BTP's integration with Cloud Foundry allows organizations to take advantage of elastic scaling capabilities, ensuring that applications can handle spikes in demand and maintain high levels of performance and availability. With Cloud Foundry's built-in resilience features, such as automatic failover and self-healing, businesses can minimize downtime and deliver a seamless user experience.

Conclusion: Unleashing the Power of Integration

The integration of SAP BTP with Cloud Foundry represents a significant milestone in the evolution of enterprise application development and deployment. By combining the strengths of these two platforms, organizations can harness the power of cloud-native technologies to drive innovation, accelerate time-to-market, and stay ahead of the competition in today's fast-paced digital economy. Whether embarking on a new digital initiative or modernizing existing systems, the synergy between SAP BTP and Cloud Foundry offers endless possibilities for organizations seeking to unlock new levels of agility, scalability, and business value.

entry.title: ABAP Integration with FastAPI for File Uploads: Seeking Code Guidance
entry.content:
Problem Statement:

I am working on integrating an ABAP system with a FastAPI server to upload files via HTTP POST requests. I have a FastAPI endpoint set up to receive file uploads, and I need assistance with creating the ABAP code to send a file to this endpoint.

Description:

  1. FastAPI Endpoint: I have a FastAPI endpoint (POST /upload/) set up to receive file uploads. The endpoint expects the file to be included in the body of the request.

  2. ABAP System: On the ABAP side, I need to write code that reads a file from the filesystem and sends it to the FastAPI endpoint via an HTTP POST request.

Requirements:

  1. HTTP POST Request: The ABAP code should construct an HTTP POST request to the FastAPI endpoint.

  2. Multipart Form Data: The file should be sent as multipart form data in the body of the POST request.

    curl -X POST -F "file=@data.json" https://endpoint.cfapps.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com/upload/ This curl works on vscode or command prompt. I want to achieve same from ABAP. 

    Server accepts data file containing JSON, does processing and returns JSON. Sever side is python.
    #if_http_client #abap #btp #httprequests #fileuploads #Post @Tomas_Buryanek I saw your answer for similar query on image upload to servicenow. Kindly help with this, seeking your expertise.

    Regards,
    Naveen

entry.title: ABAP Cloud Project Creation Error in ADT
entry.content:
Hi All, 

I am getting this weird error : You were successfully authenticated, but an error occurred during fetching the Service Instance's SAP System ID.

As i refered atleast 10 different blogs and solutions but still not able to resolve this issue

Simply, I have created abap enviroinment and try to create abap cloud project in ADT , But ending up getting this error.

I have tried booster and manually created abap enviroinment , its of no use

Need your solution ...

Screenshots : 

ABAP Cloud SAP BTP, ABAP environment ABAP Connectivity

entry.title: How to hide a result list column in a value help?
entry.content:
In BTP ABAP environment, I am making a value help based on a CDS entity.

The @UI.selectionField: [] annotation works fine, and the filters are rendered in the proper order.

The @UI.LineItem: [ { position: # } ] also works fine where it is maintained, in that the search results are displayed in the correct order.

However, the fields that do not have @UI.LineItem annotation maintained are still displayed in the result list.  They are added to the result list at the end, after all the other fields with a @UI.LineItem annotation. I have tried to maintain @UI.LineItem: [ { hidden: true } ] to these fields, but they are still displayed.

These fields are required in the CDS view entity (they are selection fields) and must not be displayed in the result list. How do I prevent these unwanted result list fields from being displayed?

entry.title: FIRST_DAY_IN_PERIOD_GET fm giving 13 months in a fiscal year instead of 12.
entry.content:
Iam using this fm to get data of a fiscal year. If i enter posting period as 8 ie, November and Year as 2023. it should get data from 1st Dec 2022  to 30th Nov 2023 ie, 12 months. Instead of this it is fetching data from 1st Nov 2022 to 30th Nov 2023 ie, 13 months. kindly help. below is the code. it is giving e_date = 1st Nov 2022 , instead of 1st Dec 2022.
* Get Billing Date
*   Posting Date YYYY
    DATA lv_gjahr TYPE bdatj.

*   Posting Period
    DATA lv_poper TYPE poper.

    lv_gjahr = im_gjahr - 1. " Previous Year of Input

*   Date of First Day determined for Period and Fiscal Year
    CALL FUNCTION 'FIRST_DAY_IN_PERIOD_GET'
      EXPORTING
        i_gjahr        = lv_gjahr
        i_periv        = 'V3' " April - March, 4 Special Periods
        i_poper        = lv_poper
      IMPORTING
        e_date         = gv_ysd
      EXCEPTIONS
        input_false    = 1
        t009_notfound  = 2
        t009b_notfound = 3
        OTHERS         = 4.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      MESSAGE ID sy-msgid TYPE sy-msgty NUMBER sy-msgno
                 WITH sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.
    ENDIF.

    IF im_poper > 2.
      lv_poper = im_poper - 2. " Last 2 Months of Input Period

    ELSE.
      lv_poper = im_poper.
    ENDIF.
    lv_gjahr = im_gjahr.

*   Date of First Day determined for Period and Fiscal Year
    CALL FUNCTION 'FIRST_DAY_IN_PERIOD_GET'
      EXPORTING
        i_gjahr        = lv_gjahr
        i_periv        = 'V3' " April - March, 4 Special Periods
        i_poper        = lv_poper
      IMPORTING
        e_date         = gv_qsd
      EXCEPTIONS
        input_false    = 1
        t009_notfound  = 2
        t009b_notfound = 3
        OTHERS         = 4.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.

entry.title: create inline a filter table with new abap syntax
entry.content:
Hi experts!

I have a table productions containing, among others, article ids. Now I want to loop over all entries for which no Article ID exists in the articleIs table. Can I do this inline with the new abap syntax. 

Something like this

 

LOOP AT filter #( productions IN articles where ArticleId not in articles-ArticleId ) INTO DATA(production).

 

Does anyone know if this is possible.

Thanks and regards,

Sebastian

entry.title: Error "OData Server return 401 - Unauthorized error" / Browser is not configured to using SPNego
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

Could you please help/support on the below error. Recently we have been facing an issue when accessing the SAP Hybrid App in IPAD, After the successful authentication when the call goes to gateway system we are getting this error. More details on the error 

Error "Retrieve metadata failed - OData Server return 401 - Unauthorized error" /

Your Browser is not configured to using SPNego

The connections is via SAP BTP and we are seeing the same error logs in cloud platform. But I am not able to see any logs in gateways system.

Kindly let me know or help me to fix this issue. This is very urgent

entry.title: How to Pass multiple fields(Non Key) in Odata query(URI)
entry.content:
Hi Odata Experts,

How can i pass multiple parameters (non-key fields along with a key field) in a Odata query(URI) in the Gateway WITHOUT defining underlying get_entitySET( ) method?

The requirment is that these non-key fields along with Key field are important to fetch data from underlying FM.

 

Thanks,

Ricky

entry.title: Change Transport Layer for a Package in BTP
entry.content:
Hello,

We have assigned incorrect transport layer to a package and would like to know if there is a way we can edit the package and change the transport layer in BTP.

Currently we are not able to edit this field.

My workaround was to create a new package with correct transport layer and move all the objects into it but it wouldn't allow it . Its not even allowing me to delete the object from the package. I get the below error. 

Appreciate your suggestions

entry.title: Change values in debugging for variables without a Pen Icon
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I want to change the value of a variable in debugging mode. I know we can change values when there is a Pen icon on right side of the Variable. But, I need to know how to change values of those variables that are without these Pen Symbol. Is it possible to change them in debugging mode ?

Below is the screenshot for reference. Thanks in Advance.

entry.title: Pricing Calculation Issues with BAPI_RFC_CALCULATE_PRICES
entry.content:
We've encountered some challenges with the BAPI_RFC_CALCULATE_PRICES function module, and I'd like to bring your attention to two specific error messages we've been encountering:

Message 1:

  • Message Class: V1
  • No: 802
  • Description: Pricing error: Field overflow

Message 2:

  • Message Class: WV
  • No: 186
  • Description: Calculation schema &1 of appl. &2 is not capable of price calculation.

If anyone has encountered these errors before or has suggestions on how to address them, please feel free to share your thoughts and experiences in this thread. Your contributions are highly valued and could significantly aid us in resolving this issue promptly.

We are mainly focusing on the Message 2, which encounters when try to post bulk pricing.

entry.title: A Waring message is displayed when creating an error message in RAP
entry.content:
Hi experts! 

I had to create a save_modified method in my Service and there creating an error message.

APPEND VALUE #( %tky = VALUE #( %is_draft  = if_abap_behv=>mk-on        
                                HeaderUuid = lv_header_uuid )           
                %msg = new_message( id         = lx_runtime->id             
                                    number     = lx_runtime->number         
                                    severity   = lx_runtime->severity_error )
) TO reported-header.

The UI displays a warning message with the option to cancel or save the dataset.

I would expect an error message and no option to save the dataset.

Does anybody knows how to achieve this?

Thanks and regards, Sebastian
